name: Automation Anywhere RPA Cloud REST API
what_it_does: Automation Anywhere cloud-native robotic process automation and AI agent REST API for enterprises to build, deploy, and manage software robots and AI automation agents — including bot deployment, process orchestration, work queue management, attended automation, and AI-powered process discovery — enabling automated business process automation, bot fleet management, human-robot collaboration, and intelligent document processing through Automation Anywhere's Automation 360 cloud platform. Enables AI agents to manage bot deployment for automation bot package deployment and version management automation, handle bot run management for bot execution trigger and monitoring automation, access work queue management for work item distribution and priority management automation, retrieve credential management for secure bot credential vault automation, manage AARI integration for attended automation and human-in-loop task automation, handle process discovery for automated process mining and automation candidate identification automation, access IQ Bot for intelligent document processing and extraction automation, retrieve analytics for bot performance and ROI reporting automation, manage migration management for legacy bot migration to cloud automation, and integrate Automation Anywhere with SAP, Salesforce, and enterprise applications for agentic process automation.
use_calls: Orchestrating enterprise bot fleets and managing work queue distribution for RPA automation agents
Deploying and versioning automation packages across environments for DevOps automation agents
Triggering attended automations and managing human-in-loop task routing for process automation agents
Processing intelligent documents with IQ Bot for unstructured data extraction automation agents
use_cases: Orchestrating enterprise bot fleets and managing work queue distribution for RPA automation agents
Deploying and versioning automation packages across environments for DevOps automation agents
Triggering attended automations and managing human-in-loop task routing for process automation agents
Processing intelligent documents with IQ Bot for unstructured data extraction automation agents
not_for: Simple task automation without enterprise scale (Automation Anywhere is enterprise RPA; simpler tools handle lightweight automation more cost-effectively)
Pure API integration without UI automation (Automation Anywhere excels at UI/screen automation; API-native platforms are simpler for pure API integration)
Startups and small businesses (enterprise pricing and complexity targets large organizations)
best_when: A large enterprise wanting AI agents to orchestrate and monitor automation bot fleets, manage intelligent document processing, and coordinate human-robot workflows through Automation Anywhere's cloud-native Automation 360 RPA platform.
avoid_when: ENTERPRISE LICENSE REQUIRED: Automation Anywhere serves large enterprises; automated open-developer assumption creates enterprise_agreement_required; Automation 360 requires enterprise contract; automated must have Automation Anywhere license. BOTS MUST BE BUILT IN AUTOMATION 360: Automation packages are developed in Automation 360 Studio; automated API-creates-bot assumption creates no_bot_to_deploy for API deployments of bots not built in the studio; automated must develop bots in Automation 360 before deploying via API. CONTROL ROOM IS CLOUD OR ON-PREM: Automation Anywhere Automation 360 is cloud; on-premises Control Room has different API; automated version-agnostic assumption creates endpoint_mismatch for cloud API calls to on-premises Control Room; automated must target correct deployment. ATTENDED AUTOMATION REQUIRES AARI: Attended automation uses AARI (Automation Anywhere Robotic Interface); automated headless assumption creates no_human_interface for attended automation requiring AARI client on end-user desktops; automated must deploy AARI for attended use cases.

auth.notes: Automation Anywhere uses OAuth2 for Automation 360 REST API. REST API with JSON. San Jose, CA HQ. Founded 2003 by Mihir Shukla, Neeti Mehta Shukla, and Ankur Kothari. Raised $1.8B+ (SoftBank, General Atlantic, Salesforce Ventures). $6.8B valuation (2021). Products: Automation 360 (cloud RPA), AARI (attended automation), IQ Bot (intelligent document processing), Process Discovery. 5,000+ enterprise customers globally. Competes with UiPath, Blue Prism, and Pega for enterprise RPA.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Community Edition: free, limited bots, development only; Production requires enterprise license
pricing.paid_tiers: Annual subscription per bot license; Automation 360 Cloud; enterprise custom pricing based on bot count and users
pricing.notes: San Jose CA. $6.8B valuation. Community Edition free. 5,000+ enterprise customers. SoftBank, Salesforce Ventures backed.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth token; Control Room APIs throttled per enterprise plan

known_agent_gotchas: AUTOMATION ID VS NAME: Bot deployment uses automation package ID, not name; automated name-based assumption creates automation_not_found for bot triggering using display names instead of IDs; automated must retrieve automation IDs via list API before triggering

BOT RUN IS ASYNC: Bot executions are asynchronous in Automation 360; automated sync-result assumption creates premature_status_check for status reads before bot completes; automated must poll deployment status until terminal state

WORK QUEUE REQUIRES DEVICE POOL: Work queue items require assigned device pool for execution; automated queue-only assumption creates no_execution for queued items without configured device pool; automated must configure device pool for work queue processing

CREDENTIAL VAULT USES LOCKER SYSTEM: Automation Anywhere credentials are organized in Credential Vault Lockers; automated flat-credential assumption creates credential_not_found for credentials not stored in the correct locker; automated must use correct locker when accessing credentials

IQ BOT REQUIRES TRAINING: IQ Bot intelligent document processing requires training with document samples; automated zero-shot assumption creates low_extraction_accuracy for IQ Bot instances not trained on document type; automated must train IQ Bot with representative document samples before production use
agent_readiness.security_notes: Enterprise RPA. SOC2, GDPR, HIPAA, FedRAMP. OAuth2. US/EU/APAC. Business process and credential vault data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Enterprise RPA. SOC2, GDPR, HIPAA, FedRAMP. OAuth2. US/EU/APAC. Process automation and enterprise credentials.
